extraction.one_line_summary: BISSEGGER Stefan won the 17.4km time trial by 0.83 seconds over CAVAGNA Rémi, taking the yellow jersey from MATTHEWS Michael who lost 23 seconds.
tactical_patterns: BISSEGGER's track background gives decisive advantage on short punchy technical TTs over pure power specialists like DENNIS Rohan.
KRAGH ANDERSEN Søren beat DENNIS at intermediate split but faded on uphill finish, revealing weakness on climbing finales.
ROGLIČ Primož losing 8 seconds in 17km TT suggests Jumbo-Visma deliberately managing effort before mountain stages.
MCNULTY Brandon's fourth place reveals UAE's strong TT depth for future stage race support roles.

rider_signals.BISSEGGER Stefan: positive: beat Roglič by 8s in technical uphill TT, track skills translate to short punchy efforts
rider_signals.CAVAGNA Rémi: positive: only 0.83s behind winner despite French champion status suggesting stage race form peaking
rider_signals.DENNIS Rohan: neutral: set early benchmark but pure power insufficient on technical uphill finish compared to punchier riders
rider_signals.KRAGH ANDERSEN Søren: neutral: fastest at intermediate split but lost 7s on final climb, revealing technical strength but climbing weakness
rider_signals.ROGLIČ Primož: neutral: third place 8s down suggests controlled ride rather than maximum effort before mountain stages
rider_signals.MCNULTY Brandon: positive: 9s down in fourth shows emerging TT strength for 22-year-old American
rider_signals.MATTHEWS Michael: negative: lost 23s and yellow jersey, confirmed as sprinter struggling on uphill TT finales
rider_signals.SCHACHMANN Maximilian: positive: sixth fastest as defending champion shows consistent TT ability at 9s down
